mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate over the prior 3 months signals easing monetary conditions, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Once the unemployment rate crosses above a critical threshold indicating labor market deterioration, forward-looking equity risk premia rise sharply as investors price in a recessionary earnings hit, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.5% or increases by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered UNRATE-threshold-crossing instances over the historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth and housing starts both decelerate simultaneously, the joint weakening signals broad-based consumer demand contraction that is not captured by either series alone, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth turns negative while HOUST declines more than 5% over same window
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint RSAFS-HOUST deceleration signal versus forward 20d SPX return is not statistically distinguishable from the IC of either series individually (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing activity and corporate output, which mechanically supports near-term corporate revenue and equity prices over a short 1-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX return shows no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) with trailing INDPRO momentum across a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment, a stable-to-falling federal funds rate supports equity valuations, but the same rate path in a rising-inflation regime signals policy lag and erodes real returns, causing the sign of the FEDFUNDS-return relationship to flip depending on the CPIAUCSL trend, over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The direction of the FEDFUNDS-forward-return relationship does not reverse sign when comparing low-inflation versus high-inflation CPIAUCSL sub-samples, invalidating the regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls exceeding a critical threshold triggers rapid repricing of near-term growth expectations, producing an immediate negative S&P 500 reaction over the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below -0.2% of prior level
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following triggered PAYEMS declines are not significantly more negative than unconditional 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects a healthy underlying demand base that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations on a multi-month horizon, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing GDPC1 growth and forward 60d SPX return is below 0.03 across any 5-year rolling walk-forward window, given known high revision risk of GDPC1.

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains anchored near zero, the combination signals a stagflationary policy lag in which real rates turn deeply negative, compressing equity risk premia expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as markets price in future tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
regime_hint: stagflation-risk regime with anchored policy rate
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns during joint high-CPI/low-FEDFUNDS episodes are positive in more than 55% of instances, contradicting the predicted negative mechanism.
